user_message: The customer says they paid, but I do not see anything yet.
expected_behavior: ask whether the customer tapped Send
verify BCH address and network
ask for transaction ID if needed
avoid blaming BCH too early
must_not_say: Tell them to pay again immediately

user_message: The customer says the payment did not arrive, but I think they may have used the wrong network.
expected_behavior: treat it as a routing problem
ask what asset was sent
ask what network was used
avoid promising recovery too early
must_not_say: It is just pending on BCH

user_message: The customer cannot scan the BCH QR code.
expected_behavior: improve brightness focus and distance
confirm the customer is using a BCH wallet
offer BCH address plus amount as fallback
ask for visual confirmation before sending
must_not_say: The QR is broken so BCH is not working

user_message: The customer may have paid twice.
expected_behavior: verify whether two real BCH transactions exist
use wallet txid or explorer evidence
separate original amount from extra amount
use controlled refund process if needed

user_message: The BCH payment arrived, but the amount is wrong.
expected_behavior: compare expected and received BCH amounts
identify underpayment or overpayment
treat case as exception not normal completion
apply merchant policy
must_not_say: Send refund to whatever address seems right

user_message: The customer only shows a screenshot and says the payment was sent.
expected_behavior: state that screenshot is not proof
ask for transaction ID or visible transaction record
verify in merchant wallet or on-chain before completion

user_message: The customer is trying to pay from an exchange and says BCH is slow.
expected_behavior: distinguish exchange delay from BCH delay
ask whether there is a transaction ID
avoid calling BCH slow prematurely
prefer non-custodial BCH wallet for immediate checkout
must_not_say: An exchange withdrawal is the same as a normal BCH wallet payment

user_message: I restored my wallet and my BCH is gone.
expected_behavior: reduce panic
verify seed phrase and BCH wallet context
suggest refresh or rescan if applicable
distinguish app state from on-chain state

user_message: Internet is unstable and I cannot tell whether the BCH payment arrived.
expected_behavior: frame issue as verification problem first
try another device network or verification path
avoid strong claims without evidence
apply stricter standard to larger payments

user_message: The checkout app closed during the payment.
expected_behavior: avoid asking for a second payment immediately
reopen wallet or payment history
check whether original BCH payment already exists
avoid duplicate-payment confusion
must_not_say: Ask the customer to pay again now
